title: Quadlets and .pod templates
body:
### Issue Description

Referenzing a templated `.pod` quadlet from a templated `.container` quadlet doesn't seem to be possible.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Create these quadlet files:

```
# foo@.pod
[Pod]

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
```
```
# foo_app@.container
[Container]
Pod=foo@%i.pod
Image=alpine
Exec=sleep %i

[Service]
Restart=always

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
```

And run `/usr/lib/systemd/system-generators/podman-system-generator --dryrun`.

### Describe the results you received

```
...
quadlet-generator[1668438]: converting "foo_app@.container": quadlet pod unit foo@%i.pod does not exist
...
```

### Describe the results you expected

A service file for the container, referenzing the pod, using `%i`, as the instance name might  be unknown.

### podman info output

comments.body:
Actually it's even worse. If one tries to work around the limitation of this bug, creation of the instantiated pod fails:
```
foo@bar-pod[1703114]: Error: running pod create option: names must match [a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9_.-]*: invalid argument
systemd[1]: foo@bar-pod.service: Control process exited, code=exited, status=125/n/a
```

Hi Thomas,

Yes Quadlet has an issue when referencing templated Quadlet files.

You can see my comment here: https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/17744#discussioncomment-9856920, though it refers to linked `.build` files, the same applies for `.pod` files.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I'm also having this issue - I have a template `tailscale@.container` with the line
```
Pod=%i.pod
```
and running the generator, I get:
```
quadlet-generator[16577]: converting "tailscale@.container": quadlet pod unit %i.pod does not exist
converting "tailscale@[my instance name].container": quadlet pod unit %i.pod does not exist
```

Hello guys!

I added a comment in https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/17744#discussioncomment-13199953, I think it can help you.
would make more sense to use `%p` rather than `%N` when generating the `systemd` service unit from the instantiated container definition.
